name: Winter Way
description: Cycle the moderate 13.3-mile Valdeorras – return of the Sil river touring route, gaining 802 feet with views of the Sil Valley.
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around O Barco de Valdeorras traverse a landscape shaped by the Sil valley, the surrounding Sierra del Eje, and the Trevinca Massif. The region features a diverse terrain, including lush river valleys, hillsides covered with vineyards, and more challenging mountainous areas. Cyclists can explore routes that follow the Sil River, wind through the Valdeorras Wine Region, or ascend towards higher elevations. Best touring cycling routes around O Barco De Valdeorras The most popular touring cycling route is Winter Way, a 13.3 miles (21.4 km) trail that takes 1 hour 37 minutes to complete. This moderate route follows a section of the historic Camino de Invierno. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is A Gabanceira loop from O Barco de Valdeorras, a difficult 27.0 miles (43.5 km) path. This route offers challenging climbs and expansive views of the surrounding area. Local touring cyclists also love the River Valley Trail – Sil River loop from O Barco de Valdeorras, a 15.1 miles (24.4 km) trail leading through the Sil River valley, often completed in about 1 hour 37 minutes. Touring cycling around O Barco de Valdeorras is defined by the Sil River, extensive vineyards, and the foothills of the Trevinca Massif. The network offers options for various ability levels, from gentle valley rides to more demanding mountain ascents. The routes in O Barco de Valdeorras are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from 2 reviews. More than 100 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore O Barco de Valdeorras's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available in O Barco de Valdeorras?
A: O Barco de Valdeorras offers over two dozen touring cycling routes. These routes cater to various skill levels, including 1 easy route, 7 moderate routes, and 15 difficult routes, ensuring options for every cyclist.

Q: Are there any easy touring cycling routes suitable for beginners or families?
A: Yes, there is at least one easy touring cycling route available. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14936245" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pazo de Arnado loop from O Barco de Valdeorras&lt;/a&gt; is a 15.1 km path that offers a gentle ride, ideal for those seeking a less strenuous experience or cycling with family.

Q: What kind of landscapes can I expect to see while cycling in O Barco de Valdeorras?
A: The region boasts diverse landscapes. You'll cycle through the lush Sil Valley, alongside the Sil River, and past extensive vineyards of the Valdeorras Wine Region. For more challenging rides, routes ascend into the foothills of the Trevinca Massif, offering panoramic views and glimpses of ancient yew forests like El Teixadal.

Q: Are there any waterfalls or unique natural features to discover on the routes?
A: Yes, the region features impressive natural sights. You can find the Pincheira de Fervenza, a 25-meter waterfall with a natural pond, offering idyllic views of the Sil Valley. Additionally, the Serra da Enciña da Lastra Natural Park, with its unique limestone summits and Mediterranean vegetation, is a distinct ecological area to explore.

Q: Can I combine cycling with wine tasting in O Barco de Valdeorras?
A: Absolutely. The Valdeorras Wine Region is famous for its Godello and Mencía wines. Many cycling routes wind through these vineyards, offering the unique opportunity to explore local wineries, visit traditional underground wine cellars known as 'covas,' and enjoy wine tastings.

Q: What historical landmarks or attractions can I visit along the cycling routes?
A: The area is rich in history. You can encounter Roman-era bridges, such as the notable one in Éntoma over the Galir River, which is part of the Camino de Invierno. Other historical sites include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3999102" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pazo de Arnado&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6553250" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ruins of the Old Church with Altar of the Virgin&lt;/a&gt;. The Old Town of O Barco itself also offers historical buildings and the Plaza Mayor.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes available?
A: Yes, several routes are designed as loops. For instance, the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31845885" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;A Gabanceira loop from O Barco de Valdeorras&lt;/a&gt; offers expansive views, while the moderate &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40012640" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;River Valley Trail – Sil River loop from O Barco de Valdeorras&lt;/a&gt; provides a scenic ride along the river.

Q: What do other touring cyclists say about the routes in O Barco de Valdeorras?
A: The touring cycling routes in O Barco de Valdeorras are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars from 2 reviews. Cyclists often praise the diverse terrain, from gentle river valleys to challenging mountain ascents, and the blend of natural beauty with cultural heritage.

Q: Is it possible to see Las Médulas while cycling in the area?
A: While Las Médulas is slightly further afield, it can be incorporated into a longer cycling tour. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40012954" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Winter Way – View of Las Médulas loop from O Barco de Valdeorras&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 46.9 km route that offers views of this UNESCO World Heritage site, known for its striking red landscape from a former Roman gold mine.

Q: What is the best time of year to go touring cycling in O Barco de Valdeorras?
A: The region's diverse landscapes, including centenary chestnut groves, are particularly vibrant and colorful during autumn. However, spring and early summer also offer pleasant weather for cycling, allowing you to enjoy the lush valleys and vineyards without extreme heat.

Q: Are there any specific points of interest or villages along the Camino de Invierno section near O Barco de Valdeorras?
A: O Barco de Valdeorras is a significant point on the Camino de Invierno. Along this historic route, you can find charming spots like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6322069" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pumares Village on the Camino de Invierno&lt;/a&gt;, which offers a blend of cultural heritage and scenic beauty for cyclists.
tips: El Pazo de Arnado or Castillo de Arnado is a manor house located in the municipality of Vilamartín de Valdeorras. El Pazo has views of the Sil river and the surrounding vineyards. Hoy en día is used for educational events and tourism.
Cycling route next to the Sil river with views of the A Pincheira park. Quiet path to ride a bike and enjoy the outdoors surrounded by native Galician forest.

Hydroelectric dam near the town of Valencia do Sil. The dam traps the waters of the Sil River to produce electricity and prevent local flooding. You can cross the dam to link the two banks of the river.
Route of recreational activities along the Sil river on Barco de Valdeorras. Quiet path to ride a bike and enjoy the free air.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle the Valdeorras – return of the Sil river route?
faqs.answer: The Valdeorras region experiences varied weather. While the trail can be enjoyed across seasons, spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant cycling conditions with milder temperatures and beautiful scenery, especially with the changing colors of the vineyards and forests. Summers can be warm, and winters might bring cooler, wetter weather.

faqs.question: Is the Valdeorras – return of the Sil river route suitable for beginner cyclists?
faqs.answer: The route is rated as moderate in difficulty. It features an orography with numerous ascents and descents, traversing a landscape shaped by the slate mountains of the Sil River. While some sections are paved, others may involve unpaved or steep segments, potentially requiring good fitness or even pushing your bike. It's not ideal for absolute beginners, but those with some cycling experience and a moderate fitness level should find it rewarding.

faqs.question: Where can I park to start the Valdeorras – return of the Sil river tour?
faqs.answer: The tour starts and ends near the Sil River in Barco de Valdeorras. You can typically find parking in or around the town, particularly near areas like Sil River – Malecón Park, Barco de Valdeorras, which is a convenient starting point.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest or viewpoints along the route?
faqs.answer: Along the route, you'll encounter several notable spots. Keep an eye out for the historic Pazo de Arnado. You'll also be treated to stunning vistas from the View of Penouta and the Sil Valley, offering panoramic views of the deep canyons and terraced vineyards. The Valencia do Sil Dam is another significant landmark.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this cycling route?
faqs.answer: The terrain is diverse and challenging. You'll cycle through a landscape of slate mountains with significant ascents and descents. Expect varied surfaces, from paved roads to unpaved segments, and even some natural trails. The region's "heroic viticulture" means vineyards cling to narrow, steep terraces, indicating considerable gradients. Some sections might be more suited for mountain biking or require good bike handling skills.

faqs.question: Does this route intersect with other well-known cycling paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route incorporates sections of several other paths. Notably, about 60% of the tour follows the Camiño de Inverno. You'll also briefly cross parts of [CIMA OU11] A Portela - A Moeda, Rúa O Bañadoiro, and the historic Camiño de Santiago.

faqs.question: Is the Valdeorras – return of the Sil river trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While specific regulations for dogs on this exact route are not detailed, generally, cycling routes in natural areas of Galicia are dog-friendly, provided dogs are kept under control, preferably on a leash, and owners clean up after them. Always be mindful of local wildlife and other cyclists.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay entrance fees for this cycling route?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to cycle the Valdeorras – return of the Sil river route itself. It's a public trail through a natural and cultural landscape. However, if you plan to visit specific wineries or historical sites along the way, they may have their own entry fees or require reservations.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete the Valdeorras – return of the Sil river tour?
faqs.answer: The full tour, covering approximately 21.4 kilometers, typically takes around 1 hour and 37 minutes to complete by touring bicycle. This duration can vary depending on your pace, stops for photos, and exploration of points of interest.

faqs.question: What makes this cycling route unique compared to others in the region?
faqs.answer: This route stands out for its blend of "heroic viticulture" with the famous Godello vineyards clinging to steep terraces, its rich historical and cultural significance with Roman and Swabian heritage, and its unique geology featuring slate mountains and rare limestone peaks. The continuous stunning views of the Sil River canyons are a major draw, offering a truly picturesque and immersive experience.

faqs.question: Are there places to eat or stay near the route?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route passes through or near Barco de Valdeorras, which offers various amenities. You'll find cafes, restaurants, and accommodation options in the town and surrounding villages. It's a good idea to check opening hours, especially in smaller, traditional villages.
